'''Novor 33''' was an uninhabitable [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Novor system/Legends|Novor system]] that orbited further from the system's [[Star/Legends|star]], [[Novor Major]], than any of the other thirty-two planets there. The planet's position saved it from destruction when Novis Major evolved into a [[Red giant/Legends|red giant]]: it expanded exponentially and engulfed the first seventeen worlds. That event reclassified the thirty-third world as the sixteenth.

==Description==
[[File:Novor.png|thumb|left|150px|The Tharin sector, highlighted]]
Novor 33<ref name="Secrets" /> was the thirty-third—later the sixteenth<ref name="Math">According to ''[[Secrets of the Sisar Run]]'', this world occupied the thirty-third orbital position. The first seventeen planets were destroyed, so 33 − 17 = 16.</ref>—of the [[Novor system/Legends|Novor system's]] thirty-three worlds overall<ref name="Secrets" /> was in both [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]]<ref name="Slice" /> and the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], within an area called the [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector]]. This placed it at galactic coordinates S-8<ref name="TEAOC" /> and on a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] called the [[Salin Corridor/Legends|Salin Corridor]]. It was an uninhabitable world, and was remarkably not affected much by the expansion of its sun.<ref name="Secrets" />

==History==
[[Time/Legends|Millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Novor system existed with thirty-three planets orbiting its star, Novor Prime. The planet known as Novor 33 was its outer-most planet. At some point, the sun expanded to become a [[Red giant/Legends|red giant]] and destroyed the seventeen worlds that orbited closest to it, leaving it with sixteen. Novor 33, nor any of the other remaining worlds, was not affected drastically by their sun's activity.<ref name="Secrets" />

Early exploits brought the ancient galactic explorers to areas near where the planet was between the years [[27,500 BBY]] and [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]].<ref name="Slice" /> The planet's [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] position placed it within the dominion of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Species/Legends|species's]] holdings at some point after the year [[25,096 BBY]].<ref name="AtXim">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]—the [[Hutt Empire]] reorganized itself sometime around [[25,000 BBY/Legends|25,000 BBY]]—maintained its political boundaries of the time for at least [[22,000 BBY/Legends|2,900 years]]. Additionally, after the invention of the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], another generation of explorers reevaluated the frontier around the planet between [[20,000 BBY/Legends|20,000 BBY]] and [[15,000 BBY/Legends|15,000 BBY]]. The area was considered explored extensively by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], at which point it was still considered within Hutt Space. This would remain the case through the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]] that raged from [[1004 BBY]] to [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]].<ref name="Slice" /> During that conflict, the Hutts and the [[Krath/Legends|Krath]] observed a non-aggression agreement.<ref name="Droids">{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}}</ref> As such, another partner of the Krath called the [[Sith Empire (Exar Kun)/Legends|Sith Empire]] had great political authority over all Hutt Space at the time.<ref name="Slice" />

The planet resided amid Hutt Space throughout the millennium after the Great Sith War, through [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="Slice" /> until the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]'' rose to power. Hutt Space shrunk greatly after this,<ref name="Comp">Comparison of maps on pages 150 (before) and 159 (after) in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]</ref> stabilizing for the long-term; the area around the Novor system and its planets fell out of Hutt influence. The [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]] lead by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]] claimed far-reaching political influence that encompassed the area where the planet resided in [[137 ABY]].<ref name="Slice" />
